Psychospiritual Coach & Energy Practitioner

There is a life you can feel that you are not yet living.

Maybe you sense it as an ache... the gap between who you are when no one is watching and who you've become to survive, to belong, to meet the expectations that were never really yours.

I am a Bridge Builder: a scientist who believes in the unseen, and a therapist who knows there is more than evidence-based practice. I stopped choosing a long time ago, and you won't have to either. I work where neuroscience, somatic intelligence, shadow work, and the deeper dimensions of human experience meet. If that speaks to you, read on…

Ways to work with me How coaching works
Is this for you

You might recognize
yourself here

People come to this work from many different starting points. What they share is a sense of being at a threshold: something in life that no longer fits, because something new is trying to emerge.

—

You've built a life that meets every external measure of success and feel strangely hollow inside it. You suspect the values you've been living aren't actually yours.

—

You feel called toward something, a different way of living, working, or being, but can't quite name it, and have no container in which to follow it.

—

You walk into rooms and feel everything. You've been told you're too sensitive, too intense, too much. But now you're beginning to wonder if your sensitivity might actually be a gift instead. (spoiler: It is!)

—

You're a therapist, a healer, a guide. You pour presence into everyone else, and there's no one pouring into you. You don't have a circle of people who can think at your depth.

—

You're done with approaches that treat you as a problem to solve. You want someone who can hold both your complexity and your capacity; someone who has walked their own version of this terrain.

—

You've had experiences: in grief, in nature, in ceremony, in a moment that arrived without warning, that your rational mind has no framework for, and you need somewhere safe to take them.

—

You've had a psychedelic experience, in a clinical setting, a ceremonial container, or on your own, and you're sitting with material that feels too significant, too strange, or too alive to process without support.

—

The shadow is calling. You can feel the pull toward the parts of yourself you've been avoiding. You know the only way through is through, and you don't want to go there alone.

This work is not a fit if you are currently in acute mental health crisis or require clinical support. California residents seeking psychotherapy may inquire separately.

Psychospiritual Coaching

When insight isn’t enough

Most of us spend years accumulating understanding about ourselves and why we do what we do. We've done the reading and named the patterns. And still, nothing moves.

That's the gap coaching works in. We work here, in the Now and in the body and parts of you that haven't gotten the memo that it's safe to change.

This isn't therapy. It's forward-facing, goal-oriented work for people who are in transition rather than in crisis. The old identity doesn't fit anymore, and they need something more rigorous than vibes, and more alive than a workbook.

What we work on together

  • Living in alignment with your actual values rather than inherited ones
  • Reclaiming the parts of yourself you abandoned to survive or belong
  • Shadow material that is quietly driving decisions you don't understand
  • The particular exhaustion of performing a version of yourself that doesn't fit
  • Meaning, purpose, and the question of what you are actually here to do
  • The becoming that waits on the other side of what frightens you most

If you're navigating the aftermath of ketamine, psilocybin, MDMA, or another plant medicine experience, I offer dedicated integration work inside the coaching container. I'm a certified ketamine-assisted psychotherapist, and trained in MDMA and psilocybin-assisted therapy too. Medicine work opens the door, but integration is where the real value of that experience gets realized. Or lost.

How it works

Sessions happen over video. Submit the intake form and you'll get a short questionnaire: what you're carrying, what you're hoping for. If my writing already resonates, that's orientation enough.

Begin the intake form

Energy Healing

Meeting what language
cannot reach

Some things do not move through talking. There are places in the body where a wound lives as sensation: a tightness that won't release, a numbness that predates memory, a current of fear that arrives before the mind has any idea why.

Your sensitivity is not a flaw. It is a nervous system processing more than most.

My sessions draw on Reiki and other energetic work, held inside something most energy healers don't have: two decades of clinical trauma training and a background in science. I'm not just channeling energy. I'm tracking your nervous system while I do it.

Sessions are offered remotely via video or phone. Skeptics are welcome. Your questioning mind is not an obstacle to this work. It is part of what makes you the kind of person this work is built for.

Book Energy Session
Psychedelic Integration

The experience was the opening.
Integration is how it becomes your life.

A psychedelic experience, in a clinical setting, a ceremonial container, or on your own, can crack open something that has needed to move for years. What happens in the weeks and months that follow determines whether that opening leads somewhere real, or slowly closes again.

Integration is not debriefing. It is the slow, careful work of allowing what was revealed, about yourself, your history, your unlived life, to actually change how you live.

The neuroplasticity window after a psychedelic experience is real, not a metaphor. And what you do with it matters.

You might be here because

  • The experience was profound and you don't want to waste it
  • The material that surfaced is complex or difficult to hold alone
  • You've been doing this work in isolation, without real integration support
  • You want to prepare well, not just show up
  • You need a practitioner who speaks both languages: clinical and ceremonial
Begin the intake form

How we work together

01. Preparation
Clarifying intention. Working with the material you're already carrying. Settling the nervous system.
02. Integration
Making meaning without flattening mystery. Bringing what was revealed into contact with your relationships, choices, and daily life.
03. Embodiment
The longer arc. Supporting the practice, not the event, as the center of this work.

Each session begins where you actually are, not where a protocol says you should be.

About

I have always lived
in two worlds.

The one that wants evidence and the one that knows things before the evidence arrives. For a long time I tried to keep them separate. I don't anymore.

My first career was in molecular biology: the language of data, controls, and replicable results. That tension drove me out of the lab and into psychology, and then into the long project of figuring out why the standard clinical model wasn't reaching what it needed to reach.

I trained as a psychotherapist, specialized in complex and developmental trauma, and founded Bay Area Mental Health, a multidisciplinary group therapy practice I built and ran for years before selling it in 2024.

I trained in somatic therapy, parts work, and ketamine, MDMA, and psilocybin-assisted therapy, not because I was collecting modalities, but because I kept finding that the most important healing was happening somewhere the clinical model wasn't designed to go.

I'm someone with graduate degrees in both molecular biology and clinical psychology who has also sat in ceremony, felt my father say goodbye the night he died, and learned to hold both of those things as real.

I am neurodivergent, and I have particular expertise working with neurodivergent clients. I write weekly in How Healing Happens on Substack. I live in the south of France with my husband, which is either a midlife crisis or a values-alignment success story, depending on who you ask.

Credentials and Expertise

  • MS, molecular biology
  • MS, clinical psychology
  • Complex & developmental trauma specialist
  • Somatic therapist · IFS-informed parts work
  • Certified ketamine-assisted psychotherapist
  • MAPS certificate in MDMA-assisted therapy
  • Trained in psilocybin-assisted therapy
  • Psychedelic integration specialist
  • Energy healing practitioner
  • Neurodivergence specialist
  • Founder, Bay Area Mental Health
